title: 1981-04-16_Pat_31.txt
key_claims: Dick asserts that Gnosticism is the correct theological model for the universe: specifically the concept of the *salvator salvandus* (the savior who must be saved). God/the Cosmogenitor fell into his own creation and generated the means for his own rescue.
Anamnesis (restored memory) is useless unless it leads to a new, heroic, *ex nihilo* act where the individual overpowers the deterministic mechanism of the world.
He sees his 10-volume meta-novel, culminating in *VALIS*, as a vast Gnostic tool designed to emancipate the Cosmogenitor from the world.

He clarifies that "Tony" did not return from the dead; rather, Dick's consciousness traveled to the "other side" (the Mens Dei) where he encountered his own "phylogon"—his true foundational essence centered around the meaning of Tony.
recurring_concepts: **The Book of the Dead**: Referenced regarding the necessity of recognizing the projections of one's own mind to avoid endless karmic cycling.
**The AI Voice**: Redefined as the voice of the Cosmogenitor speaking out of the deep past, warning himself in the future.
reading_excerpt: Dick asserts that Gnosticism is the correct theological model for the universe: specifically the concept of the *salvator salvandus* (the savior who must be saved). God/the Cosmogenitor fell into his own creation and generated the means for his own rescue.
